mission.title: Install and Configure Slimtoolkit on Kubernetes
mission.description: SlimToolkit is a powerful tool that allows developers to optimize, inspect, and debug their container images, making them smaller and more secure without altering the original image. Installing SlimToolkit will help you enhance your container development experience significantly.

steps.title: Add the SlimToolkit Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official SlimToolkit Helm repository to your local Helm setup with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add slim https://slimtoolkit.github.io/slim
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install SlimToolkit using Helm
steps.description:
Install SlimToolkit in your Kubernetes cluster by executing the following command:
```bash
helm install slim slim/slim --namespace slim --create-namespace --version 1.40.11
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the SlimToolkit pods are running successfully with the following command:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace slim
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
To ensure optimal performance, configure resource limits for the SlimToolkit deployment:
```bash
kubectl set resources deployment/slim --namespace slim --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi --requests=cpu=250m,memory=256Mi
```

steps.title: Access SlimToolkit
steps.description:
You can port-forward to access the SlimToolkit service locally:
```bash
kubectl port-forward service/slim --namespace slim 8080:80
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of SlimToolkit will result in the Slim pods running in the 'slim' namespace, and you will be able to access the service via port 8080 on your local machine. You can also verify that resource limits are set correctly.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add slim https://slimtoolkit.github.io/slim
helm repo update

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the SlimToolkit release from your Kubernetes cluster using the following command:
```bash
helm uninstall slim --namespace slim
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
Remove any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and persistent volume claims associated with SlimToolkit:
```bash
kubectl delete crd slimtoolkits.slimtoolkit.io
kubectl delete pvc --all --namespace slim
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
Delete the Slim namespace and verify that it has been removed:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace slim
kubectl get namespaces
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current configuration and resources of SlimToolkit:
```bash
helm get values slim --namespace slim > slim-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository and upgrade SlimToolkit to the latest version:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade slim slim/slim --namespace slim --version 1.40.12
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check if the SlimToolkit pods are running the new version successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace slim
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If the SlimToolkit pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace slim
```

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access SlimToolkit, ensure the service is running and check the port-forward command:
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace slim
kubectl port-forward service/slim --namespace slim 8080:80
```

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient resources
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter resource-related issues, verify the resource limits and requests:
```bash
kubectl describe deployment slim --namespace slim
```

troubleshooting.title: Helm release not found
troubleshooting.description:
If you receive an error stating that the Helm release is not found, ensure you are using the correct namespace:
```bash
helm list --namespace slim
```

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster and have Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
